dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4172 IS: Animal Freedom from Testing, Experiments, and Research Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Ms. Collins (for herself and Mr. Peters) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To amend the Animal Welfare Act to allow for the retirement of certain animals used in Federal research, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Animal Freedom from Testing, Experiments, and Research Act of 2026 or the AFTER Act of 2026.

section.header: Placement of animals used in Federal research

subsection.text: Section 14 of the Animal Welfare Act (7 U.S.C. 2144) is amended to read as follows:

paragraph.text: The term animal rescue organization means a nonprofit organization the purpose of which is to rescue covered animals and find permanent adoptive homes for those animals.
The term animal sanctuary means a nonprofit organization that—

subparagraph.text: operates a place of refuge in which—

clause.text: a covered animal is provided care for the lifetime of the animal; and
an unescorted public visitation of that animal is not permitted;

subparagraph.text: does not engage in commercial trade of covered animals;
does not permit direct contact between the public and covered animals;
does not allow the use of a covered animal for performance or exhibition purposes; and
does not conduct or permit research on a covered animal other than noninvasive behavioral research.

paragraph.text: The term animal shelter means a facility that—

clause.text: to place those animals in a permanent adoptive home; or
for purposes of law enforcement; and
engage in commercial trade of covered animals;
allow the use of a covered animal for performance or exhibition purposes; or
conduct or permit research on a covered animal other than noninvasive behavioral research.

subparagraph.text: The term covered animal means an animal that is unwanted, abandoned, or otherwise in need of placement in a home.
The term covered animal does not include—

clause.text: a rat of the genus Rattus; or
a mouse of the genus Mus.

paragraph.text: The term nonprofit organization means an organization described in section 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 and exempt from taxation under section 501(a) of that Code.
The term suitable for retirement means, with respect to an animal, that the animal has been evaluated by a licensed veterinarian and is determined to be mentally and physically healthy.

subsection.text: Any department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States that operates laboratory animal facilities or exhibits animals shall comply with the standards and other requirements promulgated by the Secretary under subsections (a), (g), (h), and (i) of section 13.

paragraph.text: Not later than 90 days after the date of enactment of the AFTER Act of 2026, any department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States operating a Federal research facility shall, after public notice and comment, promulgate regulations that, with respect to any animal of the facility that is no longer needed for research and determined to be suitable for retirement—

subparagraph.text: facilitate and encourage the adoption of the animal by, or placement of the animal with—

clause.text: an animal rescue organization, animal sanctuary, animal shelter, or individual who intends to keep the animal as a pet; or
in the case of a nonhuman primate, an animal sanctuary; and

subparagraph.text: to the maximum extent practicable, collaborate with appropriate nonprofit organizations to carry out subparagraph (A).

paragraph.text: The regulations promulgated to carry out paragraph (1)(A) shall include consideration of placing animals with the entities described in that subparagraph that are located beyond the immediate geographic vicinity of the Federal research facility at which the animal being retired is located.

subsection.text: Nothing in this section, including regulations promulgated under subsection (c)(1), shall—

paragraph.text: preempt any State or local law relating to the adoption or placement of animals used in research that is more stringent than the requirements of this section;

prohibit, prevent, forestall, or otherwise impede the placement of any chimpanzee that has been used, or was bred or purchased for use, in research conducted or supported by a Federal agency into the sanctuary system established under section 404K of the Public Health Service Act (42 U.S.C. 283m); or
prevent a State or unit of local government from adopting or enforcing an animal welfare law that is more stringent than this section.

subsection.text: Section 13 of the Animal Welfare Act (7 U.S.C. 2143) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (g) and (h) as subsections (h) and (i), respectively; and
by redesignating the second subsection (f) (relating to the certification requirement for the delivery of any animal) as subsection (g).
